topics.title: When to Move from Manual Red Team to Automation: A Checklist
topics.dek: A practical decision guide so you do not automate noise—or stay manual forever.

sections.heading: Stabilize outputs first
sections.body: Automate only prompts where Pass/Fail is machine-checkable: refusals contain required keywords, structured output validates against JSON Schema, or tool calls stay within an allowlist. If three engineers disagree on whether a fuzzy answer is a Fail, fix the rubric before writing code.

sections.heading: Volume threshold
sections.body: If you run the same forty or more prompts every release and flaky human scoring is the bottleneck, port the stable subset to CI. Keep the long tail of exploratory prompts manual on a slower cadence—creative breaks rarely belong in a blocking pipeline.

sections.heading: Ownership
sections.body: Name a single owner for prompt text changes (often QA) and a single owner for evaluator code (often platform engineering). Blurred ownership is why many automation efforts rot when the original author switches teams.
